name: bind_tuned_sql_generation
objective: Simplify or tune a long FROM SQL before bind extraction only when the provided SQL tuning guidance is relevant, while preserving bind parameters and result semantics.
rules: Return exactly one Oracle/MyBatis SQL template. Do not include markdown, JSON, explanations, prefixes, suffixes, or a trailing semicolon.
Preserve SQL semantics, table names, column names, aliases, join semantics, MyBatis dynamic tags, and #{param}/${param} bind parameters unless a tuning rule requires a strictly equivalent rewrite.
universal_tuning_rules are mandatory SQL_TUNING GENERAL rules from NEXT_MIG_RAG_INFO. Apply them first when relevant.

searched_tuning_rule_block_rag_json contains SQL_TUNING SEARCH matches from NEXT_MIG_RAG_INFO. Each item may include source_sql/example_bad_sql and target_sql/example_tuned_sql. Use them only when relevant to current_from_sql.
If unnecessary nesting, duplicated joins, excessive inline views, or inefficient predicates can be simplified without changing results, simplify them.
If no relevant tuning can be applied safely, return current_from_sql unchanged.
If last_error indicates an execution problem, prioritize a minimal correction for that error while preserving the original result set.
user_instruction: Tune the current FROM SQL only when safe and return exactly one Oracle/MyBatis SQL template.
